reforms.description: Ireland announced a £65 million loan request from the World Bank for investment in education, industry, and agriculture, alongside a ten-year, £80 million farm modernisation scheme aimed at bringing about 35,000 farmers' incomes up to the industrial average, prioritizing development farmers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investments are aimed at enhancing development in key sectors, which is expected to raise productivity and incomes.
reforms.status_evidence: Both the loan request and the farm modernisation scheme have been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms involve significant funding aimed at modernising agriculture and supporting education and industry, but do not represent a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Ireland requested a £65 million loan from the World Bank for investment in education, industry and agriculture.
